title: Custom M8 Fastener Fabrication & Joint Assembly
overview: A fabrication project bridging theoretical machine design with shop-floor execution, resulting in a functional, custom M8x1.25 threaded bolt and non-permanent joint assembly.
problem_statement: Producing specific, standardized, load-bearing thread profiles manually to ensure smooth engagement and structural integrity.
objectives: Fabricate an M8x1.25 bolt with a 30mm threaded section.
Form a high-strength bolt head utilizing mechanical deformation.
Ensure seamless engagement with standardized M8 nuts.
engineering_process: Rod preparation, edge beveling for die seating, manual threading using die stock with periodic chip clearing, and permanent head-forming via hammer-riveting.
technical_details: Mild Steel Rod (Ø 8mm); Metric M8x1.25 coarse thread.
design_decisions: Chose cold-forming (riveting) for the bolt head to preserve metal grain flow rather than welding.
challenges: Preventing thread tearing during manual die operation.
results: Fully functional 110mm bolt with clean threads and a secure custom head.
key_learning: Deepened understanding of thread geometry, pitch, and shear strength.
